question_text: Which of the following is the correct symbol for a variable resistor in a circuit diagram?

options.label: A
options.text: A rectangle with a diagonal line through it

options.label: B
options.text: A circle with an X inside

options.label: D
options.text: A rectangle with two parallel lines

explanation: A variable resistor (or rheostat) is shown as a rectangle with a diagonal arrow through it in circuit diagrams. This symbol helps us identify components that can change resistance in a circuit.
